metadata.dispatch_line: Protection order violation reported at 1457 Oakwood Drive, victim requesting immediate assistance, suspect is known to the victim

scenario.dispatch_line: Protection order violation reported at 1457 Oakwood Drive, victim requesting immediate assistance, suspect is known to the victim

facts.protection_order_violation_01: No, the suspect did not use firearms during the incident.
facts.protection_order_violation_02: Yes, the suspect used firearms in a previous incident on February 10, 2022.
facts.protection_order_violation_03: Yes, the suspect made implied threats to use firearms during the incident.
facts.protection_order_violation_04: Yes, the suspect made real threats to use firearms in previous incidents.
facts.protection_order_violation_05: Yes, the suspect has access to firearms, specifically a Glock 19 located at 1456 Oakwood Drive, Apartment 3B, Denver, CO 80210.
facts.protection_order_violation_06: Yes, the victim expressed fear about the suspect's access to firearms, which was captured in the report as 'the victim stated she was in fear for her life due to the suspect's history of violence and access to firearms'.
facts.protection_order_violation_07: No, firearms were not confiscated at the scene, but a search warrant is pending to confiscate the firearm from the suspect's address.
facts.protection_order_violation_08: Yes, the suspect is prohibited from possessing firearms due to a conviction for a misdemeanor crime of domestic violence on January 5, 2020.
facts.protection_order_violation_09: Yes, the suspect is prohibited from possessing firearms due to a qualifying order of protection issued on March 15, 2022.
facts.protection_order_violation_10: No, the suspect is not prohibited from possessing firearms due to dishonorable discharge from the military.
facts.protection_order_violation_11: Yes, the relationship of the parties is identified as intimate partners, specifically boyfriend and girlfriend.
facts.protection_order_violation_12: The history of the relationship includes frequent incidents of violence, intimidation, and threats, with the most recent incident occurring on April 1, 2023.
facts.protection_order_violation_13: Yes, all witnesses, including the neighbor, were interviewed and documented.
facts.protection_order_violation_14: Yes, a kitchen knife was used as a weapon by the suspect.
facts.protection_order_violation_15: The victim reported feeling 'terrified' and 'helpless' during the incident.
facts.protection_order_violation_16: Evidence collected includes a torn piece of clothing, a broken cell phone, and a witness statement.
facts.protection_order_violation_17: Yes, evidence of fear is articulated in the report, specifically the victim's statement that 'she was in fear for her life'.
facts.protection_order_violation_18: Yes, all threats, including the implied threat to use firearms, have been clearly documented.
facts.protection_order_violation_19: Yes, stalking behaviors, including repeated calling and sending unwanted gifts, have been identified.
facts.protection_order_violation_20: Yes, the use of coercion and/or force is articulated in the report, specifically the suspect's use of physical force to restrain the victim.
facts.protection_order_violation_21: Yes, all injuries, including visible bruises and non-visible emotional trauma, have been documented.
facts.protection_order_violation_22: Yes, there was property damage, including a broken cell phone and a torn piece of clothing.
facts.protection_order_violation_23: Yes, the victim reported being strangled, which was described in detail as 'the suspect put his hands around her neck and squeezed'. EMS was summoned due to the reported strangulation.
facts.protection_order_violation_24: Yes, the victim needed medical attention and was transported to the hospital for treatment.
facts.protection_order_violation_25: Yes, all spontaneous statements, including the victim's statement that 'she was in fear for her life', have been captured and documented.
facts.protection_order_violation_26: Yes, information about local service providers, including the National Domestic Violence Hotline, was provided to the victim.
facts.protection_order_violation_27: Yes, a threat/risk assessment tool was used with the victim to assess her risk of further violence.
facts.protection_order_violation_28: Yes, the officer gathered comprehensive information about the suspect, including his criminal history and previous incidents of violence.
facts.protection_order_violation_29: Yes, an arrest was made, and the suspect was taken into custody. The suspect was read his Miranda rights prior to questioning.
facts.protection_order_violation_30: The suspect reported feeling 'angry' and 'defensive' during the incident.
facts.protection_order_violation_31: Yes, a threat/risk assessment tool was used with the suspect to assess his risk of further violence.
facts.protection_order_violation_32: Yes, the proper protocol was followed when the suspect was not on the scene, including obtaining a search warrant to confiscate the firearm.
facts.protection_order_violation_33: Yes, there is an active warrant out for the suspect's arrest for a previous incident of domestic violence.
facts.protection_order_violation_34: Yes, the suspect has been the respondent to a protection order in the past, specifically on January 10, 2020.
facts.protection_order_violation_35: Yes, the time of the call is recorded, including the time of incident (02:45 AM), time of dispatch (02:50 AM), and time of arrival (03:05 AM).
facts.protection_order_violation_36: Yes, the scene is concisely described and diagramed, including the location of the suspect's vehicle and the victim's residence.
facts.protection_order_violation_37: Yes, photos were taken and details recorded, including the victim's injuries and the damage to the residence.
facts.protection_order_violation_38: Yes, the elements of the crime(s) are articulated to meet state and/or federal laws, specifically the elements of domestic violence and violation of a protection order.

interview_transcript.content: Can you tell me what happened, starting at the beginning?

(nervously) Oh, okay... I'll try. It's just been really scary and overwhelming. (pausing to collect my thoughts) I think it started a few days ago, when my ex-boyfriend... (hesitating) ...when he started calling me repeatedly and sending me gifts. I had already gotten a protection order against him, but he just wouldn't leave me alone. (my voice starts to shake) And then, last night... he showed up at my apartment. I was so scared. He was angry and yelling, and... (taking a deep breath) ...he had a kitchen knife. He used it to... to threaten me. (my eyes well up with tears) He put his hands around my neck and squeezed. I thought I was going to pass out. I was so afraid he was going to kill me. (sobbing) I'm just so glad the police came when they did. They saved me. (composing myself slightly) I don't know what would have happened if they hadn't gotten there when they did.

(sniffling) His name is... (pausing, hesitant to say it out loud) ...my ex-boyfriend's name is... (taking a deep breath) ...I don't think I'm supposed to say his name, is that right? (looking at you uncertainly) I mean, I've already told the other officer, but... (trailing off) Oh, I'm sorry. His name is... (whispering) the same person who has been harassing me and violating the protection order. (looking down, tears welling up in my eyes again)

(nervously) Y-yes... I think it's... (pausing, trying to remember) 1456 Oakwood Drive, Apartment 3B, in Denver. That's where he's been staying, I think. (looking up at you) He's been keeping a gun there, too. A Glock 19. I'm scared he's going to use it to hurt me. (my voice trembling) That's one of the reasons I was so afraid last night. He didn't have the gun with him, but... (shuddering) he implied that he might use it if I didn't do what he wanted.
Can you describe his appearance for me?

(thinking for a moment) He's... um... about 5'10" or 5'11" with a medium build. He has short, dark brown hair and brown eyes. He usually has a bit of stubble on his face, but sometimes he shaves. (pausing, trying to remember any distinctive features) He has a scar above his left eyebrow, from a fight we got into a few years ago. And he has a tattoo of a snake on his right forearm. (shuddering slightly at the memory) I've seen him wear a silver necklace with a little pendant, but I don't know what it is. He usually wears jeans and a t-shirt or a hoodie. (looking down, my voice barely above a whisper) I'm sorry, I don't know if that helps...
Are there any other witnesses of what he did last night?

(nodding) Yes, my neighbor... she saw some of what happened. She lives in the apartment next door, and she heard us arguing. She came over and saw him... (shuddering) ...with the knife, and she called the police. I'm so grateful she did. I don't know what would have happened if she hadn't intervened. (tearfully) She was really brave, and I'm just so thankful she was there for me. The police talked to her, and she gave them a statement. I think they also got some other evidence from the scene... like my broken phone and some torn clothes. (sniffling) It was all just so scary and overwhelming...

narrative: Last Night, the victim's ex boyfriend came to the victim's house and allegedly brandished a knife and threatened her life with a gun, kept at his residence in Denver. The neighbor who filed a police report was a victim to the crime.
